title: hologyne lauterbachiana
intro: Hologyne lauterbachiana, also known as the 'Milkflower' or 'White Milkflower', is a rare and endangered plant species native to the island of New Guinea. It is a flowering plant characterized by its white, star-shaped flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing due to its active compounds. Herbal preparations of Hologyne lauterbachiana are often made into infusions, tinctures, or ointments to utilize its medicinal properties, although the plant is currently protected and its use is subject to strict regulations.
